title: How to suppress 'warning: Timing checks are not supported.' warnings?
body:
### Discussed in https://github.com/steveicarus/iverilog/discussions/1207
Originally posted by **zapta** January 26, 2025 When we run the command below, we get tens fo 'Timing checks are not supported.' warnings. Is there a flag or a similar way to suppress them, without modifying the source file or running post processing filters? ``` iverilog -g2012 -o _build/blinky_tb.out -DVCD_OUTPUT=_build/blinky_tb -I"/Users/user/.apio/packages/oss-cad-suite/share/yosys/gowin" "/Users/user/.apio/packages/oss-cad-suite/share/yosys/gowin/cells_sim.v" blinky.v blinky_tb.v /Users/user/.apio/packages/oss-cad-suite/share/yosys/gowin/cells_sim.v:176: warning: Timing checks are not supported. /Users/user/.apio/packages/oss-cad-suite/share/yosys/gowin/cells_sim.v:190: warning: Timing checks are not supported. /Users/user/.apio/packages/oss-cad-suite/share/yosys/gowin/cells_sim.v:191: warning: Timing checks are not supported. /Users/user/.apio/packages/oss-cad-suite/share/yosys/gowin/cells_sim.v:207: warning: Timing checks are not supported. /Users/user/.apio/packages/oss-cad-suite/share/yosys/gowin/cells_sim.v:208: warning: Timing checks are not supported. /Users/user/.apio/packages/oss-cad-suite/share/yosys/gowin/cells_sim.v:226: warning: Timing checks are not supported. /Users/user/.apio/packages/oss-cad-suite/share/yosys/gowin/cells_sim.v:227: warning: Timing checks are not supported. /Users/user/.apio/packages/oss-cad-suite/share/yosys/gowin/cells_sim.v:228: warning: Timing checks are not supported. /Users/user/.apio/packages/oss-cad-suite/share/yosys/gowin/cells_sim.v:246: warning: Timing checks are not supported. /Users/user/.apio/packages/oss-cad-suite/share/yosys/gowin/cells_sim.v:247: warning: Timing checks are not supported. ... ``` The first warning is about the $setup() function below ``` (* abc9_flop, lib_whitebox *) module DFF (output reg Q, input CLK, D); parameter [0:0] INIT = 1'b0; initial Q = INIT; specify (posedge CLK => (Q : D)) = (480, 660); $setup(D, posedge CLK, 576); endspecify always @(posedge CLK) Q <= D; endmodule ```
